paragraph: Wyckoff's single-family market is a real seller's market right now. With 75 sales over eight months at a median of $1,275,000, homes are moving fast, many in multiple offer situations. The sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.04, meaning homes are selling above asking more than six out of ten times, and the median days on market is 14. Sellers are dictating terms here, and anything priced competitively will draw serious interest. The condo and townhouse market, by contrast, is sparse with only six sales at a median of $951,400, which makes month-to-month movement harder to read, but the data shows similar seller-friendly conditions: homes selling above asking about two-thirds of the time and moving in roughly two weeks. If you're looking at Wyckoff, understand that single-family is where the volume and liquidity are. The condo tier exists but remains a niche category. The single-family market rewards priced homes with speed and competition, so if you're selling, price accordingly to asking price expectations. If you're buying, you'll need to move decisively and be prepared for multiple offers on anything that hits the market in move-in condition.
